designConstraints: All layouts must conform to the 8px spacing grid system (scale: 4, 8, 12, 16, 24, 32, 48, 64px)
12-column grid with 24px gutters and 24px page margins; max content width 1280px
Dark-first color palette: background-primary (#0F172A), surface-primary (#1E293B), surface-elevated (#293548)
Primary text uses silver-text (#CBD5E1) on dark surfaces; secondary text uses muted-text (#64748B)
Gold-amber (#F59E0B) reserved exclusively for CTA elements, focus rings, warning states, and positive trend indicators
Teal-success (#14B8A6) used for on-track/positive budget health indicators and success states
Coral-error (#F87171) used for over-budget states, negative trend indicators, and error states
Card components use elevation level 1 (0 1px 4px rgba(0,0,0,0.3)) on navy-surface background
Dropdowns, chart tooltips, and popovers use elevation level 2 (0 4px 16px rgba(0,0,0,0.4))
Border radius: small=6px for chips/badges, medium=10px for cards and inputs, large=16px for primary containers, pill=9999px for status badges

Typography hierarchy: heading-1 (DM Sans 24px/700) for page title, heading-2 (DM Sans 16px/600) for card headers, heading-3 (DM Sans 15px/500) for sub-sections, body (DM Mono 14px/400) for data values, label (DM Mono 12px/500) for metadata, small (DM Mono 11px/400) for supplementary text
DM Mono used for all numeric/financial data display to ensure monospaced alignment across stat cells and ranked lists
Maximum 3 levels of visual hierarchy per card component
All interactive touch targets must meet minimum 44x44px dimensions
Chart toggle (bar/line) must be visually distinct with active state using gold-amber accent
ExportCSVButton must be right-aligned below all card content, consistent with dashboard action placement patterns
NoDataEmptyState component must occupy the same spatial footprint as the content it replaces to prevent layout shift
Motion: page transitions use fade at 300ms normal duration; chart type toggle animation uses 150ms fast duration with cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1) easing
Disabled elements render at 0.38 opacity using text-disabled (muted-text token)
Focus ring: 2px solid gold-amber (#F59E0B) with 2px offset on all interactive elements
Z-index layering: chart tooltips at dropdown level (1000), MonthSelector dropdown at dropdown level (1000), SettingsDialog at modal level (1200)
Desktop-first layout at 1440px viewport; tablet breakpoint at 768px collapses multi-column card rows to single column
AverageDashedLine on DailySpendingChart must use a visually distinct dash pattern and muted-text color to differentiate from data series
TrendIndicator arrows/icons must use teal-success for positive trends and coral-error for negative trends with accompanying percentage label
SpendingDiffBadge uses pill border-radius with semantic color fill based on comparison outcome (teal-success/coral-error/gold-amber)
referencePatterns: Three-stat summary header pattern (StatCell grid): horizontal row of 3 equal-width stat cells with large numeric value, label, and TrendIndicator — consistent with fintech dashboard KPI headers

Card-based analytical layout: each major section (period summary, daily chart, top categories, biggest expenses, month comparison) wrapped in individual surface-primary cards with consistent 24px internal padding
Toggleable chart type pattern: ChartTypeToggle uses segmented control with active state indicator (gold-amber underline or background fill) — mirrors tab active state pattern from NavigationTabs

Stacked bar with per-row progress indicators: TopCategoriesCard uses StackedCategoryBar at top followed by CategoryDetailRow list items each containing a mini progress bar — consistent with BudgetSummaryCard ProgressBar pattern on Dashboard

Ranked list pattern: BiggestExpensesCard uses RankedExpenseRow with ordinal rank indicator, merchant name, category chip, and right-aligned amount — mirrors RecentExpensesList/ExpenseRow pattern from Dashboard

Month-over-month comparison card: ComparisonBars display two horizontal bars (current vs previous month) with SpendingDiffBadge summarizing delta — consistent with BudgetStatusBadge semantic color usage
MonthSelector navigation bar: reused from Dashboard and Add Expense pages — chevron left/right controls with month/year label centered in TopBar area
Filter and navigation chrome: TopBar + NavigationTabs + MonthSelector + SettingsDialog reused identically from Dashboard page for consistent shell
Empty state pattern: NoDataEmptyState uses centered illustration with heading-2 title and body-text description, matching EmptyState component pattern from Dashboard
Export action placement: right-aligned utility button below primary content area, using secondary button variant to avoid competing with primary CTAs
Chart tooltip pattern: ChartTooltip appears on hover/focus with elevation level 2 shadow, surface-elevated background, and formatted currency value using DM Mono
Category color coding: CategoryDetailRow and StackedCategoryBar segments use Category.color_hex values from the Category data model for visual consistency with Dashboard donut chart
accessibilityRequirements: WCAG 2.1 AA color contrast ratio of 4.5:1 minimum for all body text; silver-text (#CBD5E1) on deep-navy (#0F172A) achieves approximately 10.9:1
WCAG 2.1 AA color contrast ratio of 3:1 minimum for large text (heading-1 24px/700) and UI components
Muted-text (#64748B) on deep-navy (#0F172A) must be verified for AA compliance on non-text UI components; use silver-text for critical labels if contrast is insufficient

All chart data (DailySpendingChart, StackedCategoryBar, ComparisonBars) must provide non-visual alternatives: ARIA labels describing the data summary, and a visually hidden data table or description for screen readers
DailySpendingChart must not rely solely on color to distinguish bar vs line chart types; use shape/pattern differentiation
TrendIndicator must convey direction via both color and icon/text (not color alone) to support color-blind users
ChartTypeToggle must use role='group' with aria-label='Chart type' and individual radio-style buttons with aria-pressed or aria-checked states
AverageDashedLine must be identified with an aria-label or legend entry; not conveyed by dash pattern alone
StatCell components must use semantic heading structure or aria-labelledby to associate stat values with their labels
RankedExpenseRow list must be wrapped in a <ol> (ordered list) element with each row as <li> to convey ranking semantically
CategoryDetailRow list must be wrapped in a <ul> element with appropriate list semantics
ExportCSVButton must have a descriptive aria-label: 'Export expenses for [Month Year] as CSV'
MonthSelector navigation controls must have aria-label='Previous month' and aria-label='Next month' on chevron buttons
All interactive elements (ChartTypeToggle, ExportCSVButton, MonthSelector, SettingsDialog trigger) must be fully keyboard navigable with visible focus indicators using 2px gold-amber focus ring
ChartTooltip must be accessible via keyboard focus on chart data points, not only mouse hover; use role='tooltip' with aria-describedby association
SpendingDiffBadge must include screen-reader text describing the comparison outcome (e.g., '15% more than last month')
NoDataEmptyState must be announced to screen readers when it replaces chart/list content; use aria-live='polite' region for dynamic content updates
SettingsDialog must trap focus when open, use role='dialog' with aria-modal='true' and aria-labelledby pointing to dialog title
NavigationTabs must use role='tablist' with individual role='tab' elements and aria-selected state
Minimum touch target size of 44x44px enforced on all interactive elements including ChartTypeToggle segments, MonthSelector chevrons, and RankedExpenseRow items
Page must have a logical heading hierarchy: h1 for page title, h2 for card section headers, h3 for sub-sections within cards
Motion animations must respect prefers-reduced-motion media query; chart transitions and page fades should be disabled or reduced when user preference is set
dataModelDependencies: Expense entity: id, amount, merchant, category_id, date, payment_method_id, note, currency — used by BiggestExpensesCard (RankedExpenseRow) and CSV export
Category entity: id, name, color_token, color_hex, icon — used by TopCategoriesCard (CategoryDetailRow, StackedCategoryBar) and RankedExpenseRow category chip
Budget entity: id, month, year, amount, currency — used by PeriodSummaryHeader for budget vs actual comparison context
DailyAggregate entity: id, date, total_amount, transaction_count, month, year — used by DailySpendingChart and AverageDashedLine calculation
CategoryAggregate entity: id, category_id, month, year, total_amount, transaction_count, percentage — used by TopCategoriesCard (StackedCategoryBar, CategoryDetailRow progress indicators)

MonthlyComparison schema (from get-monthly-summary endpoint): total spent, daily average, transaction count, comparison deltas vs previous month — used by PeriodSummaryHeader (StatCell, TrendIndicator) and MonthComparisonCard (ComparisonBars, SpendingDiffBadge)

UserSettings entity: id, currency, default_monthly_budget, locale — used globally for currency formatting across all monetary displays (StatCell amounts, RankedExpenseRow amounts, ComparisonBars labels)
QuickAddSuggestion entity: not directly consumed by Spending Insights page
PaymentMethod entity: not directly consumed by Spending Insights page display (referenced indirectly via Expense records)
